sentence_reviews.text: Knowledge editing changes selected factual or behavioral associations in parameters or external memory while attempting to preserve unrelated capabilities.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA and Deep Learning support learning and knowledge representation broadly, but not model editing that preserves unrelated behavior.
sentence_reviews.notes: Needs a model editing source such as ROME/MEMIT or knowledge-editing survey.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Knowledge editing & memory consolidation is to connect Parametric memory, Continual learning & test-time adaptation pipelines to downstream capabilities including Memory lifecycle, forgetting & consolidation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support several underlying concepts, but the downstream capability list is atlas-derived rather than directly evidenced.
sentence_reviews.notes: Treat as a graph-positioning claim; dependencies are plausible but the full downstream list needs direct source support.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Memory lifecycle, forgetting & consolidation
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support prerequisite concepts, but not this exact downstream substrate relationship.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an atlas graph relationship claim and should cite the downstream system if exact verification is needed.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support prerequisite concepts, but not this exact downstream substrate relationship.

sentence_reviews.text: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include Parametric memory, Continual learning & test-time adaptation pipelines.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The stale/private/contradictory/incorrectly ranked limitation is generally plausible for memory and retrieval systems, but most packets cite only broad background sources.
sentence_reviews.notes: The limitation should be narrowed or backed by a memory/retrieval safety source; dependency list portion is graph-derived.

sentence_reviews.text: Knowledge editing & memory consolidation stores, selects, or obtains information through Parametric memory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The packet references support parametric_memory or the node mechanism broadly, but the dependency rationale is graph-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship is plausible but should be verified with a dependency-specific source for exact wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Knowledge editing & memory consolidation uses Continual learning & test-time adaptation pipelines as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The packet references support continual_testtime_adaptation or the node mechanism broadly, but the dependency rationale is graph-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship is plausible but should be verified with a dependency-specific source for exact wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Knowledge editing & memory consolidation uses Causal inference as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The packet references support causal_inference or the node mechanism broadly, but the dependency rationale is graph-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship is plausible but should be verified with a dependency-specific source for exact wording.

edge_reviews.notes: Knowledge editing modifies or uses parametric memory, but “retrieves_from” is not the right relationship type.
The dependency is central to the node mechanism as described; source support is conceptual rather than exact.
Causal tracing/inference is used in some editing methods, but not a general common dependency from cited sources.
